description: In Python, a clean build does not prevent AttributeError, ImportError, and TypeError that only surface at runtime since hints are not enforced.
body: In Python, passing import-time evaluation plus python -m py_compile on changed modules says nothing about runtime: AttributeError, ImportError, and TypeError that only surface at runtime since hints are not enforced surface only when the code actually runs. Use when a change touches control flow, IO, concurrency, or data shapes that the build cannot prove safe. Do not use a green compile as evidence that behavior is correct, and avoid shipping without exercising the path. Actions: run pytest on the changed package inside the project venv, reproduce the runtime path, and where relevant run tracemalloc and reference-cycle inspection to catch latent defects. Validation signals include the test/trace logs, a clean tracemalloc and reference-cycle inspection run, and observed correct output on representative input. Risk boundary: escalate or hold the change when the failure mode is data loss or memory corruption you cannot reproduce locally.
